name: Fig Rum Loaf
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Dried figs, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Generously grease a 9 X 5 inch loaf pan for each loaf.
3. Line the bottom and sides of the pan with waxed paper.
4. Combine figs, sugar, butter, and salt in a large bowl fitted with an electric mixer.
5. Add boiling water. Let cool to room temperature (butter does not have to melt).
6. In a medium bowl, sift flour, baking soda, and baking powder.
7. Add the flour mixture, egg, rum, and orange peel to the fig mixture and beat with the electric mixer at medium speed until well blended.
8. Stir in nuts.
9. Transfer batter to prepared pans, smoothing the top.
10. Bake until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean, about 65 to 75 minutes.
11. Let cool in pans on racks for 10 minutes.
12. Remove loaves from pans and discard waxed paper. Let cool completely on racks.
13. Wrap airtight and let stand at least 24 hours before serving.
Comments: Really do let age before you eat it; much better on the second day. This really is tasty and is hard not to snack away.
